👉 Else, I’ll move to the next one

Loyalty is meant for family, friends & country — not for a credit card.

I still hope rationality prevails and HDFC Bank brings reasonable criteria for the great Infinia 🤞
I have a 17-year banking relationship with HDFC Bank and will continue — as long as it remains mutually rewarding and Infinia is continued. 

Else, I will move my entire banking relationship to HSBC as my primary account 💼

Customer loyalty should be valued — not taken for granted.

Hope customer feedback is taken positively.

items.title:
Guys, looks like HDFC has dropped the ₹3L spend target (for Mar–Apr 2026) that was earlier given to me to continue with my Infinia Credit Card 🙌

And honestly, I appreciate this gesture. 👍 (I have not even spent Rs.2 Lacs in the given period till now.)

🤔 Not sure if this Mar–Apr spend target waiver is for everyone or selective  
👉 Do comment if you also received a similar email

👉Like many Infinia users, I was asked to spend ₹3L across March & April 2026, failing which the card was to be closed/downgraded.

📩 Now I’ve received an email confirming:
✅ This spend condition has been waived  
✅ My Infinia will continue till March 2027

However, post that, the original renewal conditions still remain:

👉 Spend ₹18L in FY 2026–27 OR  
👉 Maintain ₹50L in Savings/FD

And frankly, neither works for me:

❌ I don’t have ₹18L worth of spends to route via Infinia  
(And I’m definitely not increasing expenses just to keep a credit card)

❌ Parking ₹50L in Savings/FD doesn’t make sense  
(The opportunity cost is far higher than the card’s benefits)

So for now, I’ll continue enjoying the card till 2027 — and thereafter will move to another card if HDFC persists with these conditions.🤷‍♂️

items.title:
Many people—especially those who’ve recently acquired HDFC Infinia and haven’t experienced Golden era of Infinia or those who are aspiring to get Infinia — have almost elevated this card to “God status” in the credit card world 🙏💳

But let’s be honest… that perception is a bit outdated.

Probably, this kind of blind following has probably given HDFC Bank the confidence to make bold moves like:
👉 Increasing TRV requirement to ₹50 Lakhs (Savings/Current/FD)
👉 Or ₹18 Lakhs annual spend

⸻

💡 Reality Check: Is Infinia still that great?

👉 Infinia shines ONLY on SmartBuy spends
Outside SmartBuy, the story is very different:

🔹 Base reward rate: ~3.33% and even that also only applicable with 4 transfer partners who have 1:1 transfer ratio. For other transfer partner (with 2:1 ratio), the reward rate further decreases to just 1.66% 😒

That’s… honestly below average in 2026.

⸻

🔄 For the last 3–4 years, most of the efficient credit card users are using Infinia only for SmartBuy. 

✅ And use other cards 👉 for everything else (for example Magnus when it had 5:4, Magnus Burgundy now, Atlas Card, even Diners Club Metal due to its additional milestone benefits).

⸻

⚠️ Brutal Truth

A 3.33% reward rate today is very mediocre 😐

👉Axis Atlas even without Accor, gives 4% rewards and upto 5.66% rewards with milestone bonuses with 15+ transfer partners still at 2:1 ratio. Must higher than 3.33% of Infinia with just 4 partners at 1:1 ratio

👉Magnus for Burgundy gives 4.8% to 14% base rewards

👉HSBC Premier gives 6%+ base rewards if redeemed on Accor

👉Even HDFC Diners Club Metal gives 5.83% reward rate if includes milestone bonuses

🤔 Or should I say thet even some entry-level cards are delivering better real cashback value 🤔🤔🤔

⸻

🧠 I do and will continue to use Infinia only for SmartBuy and will use Atlas (or other cards) for other online & offline spend at current reward rates.  

⸻

🤝 Final Thought

I’ll be genuinely happy if HDFC:
✅ Improves base reward rate
✅ Or introduces meaningful milestone rewards

Until then… the “God Card” narrative doesn’t really hold, at least for me 

⸻

💬 Open for counter arguments!
If you’re using Infinia outside SmartBuy, I’d genuinely like to understand your reasoning 👇

⸻

Hello Everyone👋

Today feels very special

On January 6, 2026, we launched our Consultancy Services with one simple intention: to genuinely help. 

We made a conscious decision not to promote it for the first 90 days, choosing instead to observe what would happen organically.

Today, on April 5, we complete those first 90 days

And the response has been beyond anything we expected.

Here’s what you made possible:

The Inner Circle: 7

Rewards Concierge: 12

Miles to Memories: 5

Rewards Tune-Up: 18

Inbox Doctor: 4

First Paycheck Pack: 2

These are not just numbers. Each one represents a conversation, a decision, a milestone. From helping choose the right credit card for a major purchase, to planning family vacations, to guiding someone to get their first credit card on first year of their job, we’ve had the privilege of being part of so many meaningful journeys.

We’ve always believed that knowledge should be accessible to everyone. That belief led to building @TechnoFino Community into one of the largest communities around banking, credit cards, and loyalty programs in India.

But we also understand that information can feel overwhelming. We’ve experienced it ourselves. And that’s exactly why this exists.

If you ever feel stuck, unsure about your next card, confused about how to maximize your rewards, or simply want clarity, we’re here to help you move forward with confidence.

You can explore and book your consultancy here:
https://technofino.in/packages/

Thank you for your trust. It means more than words can ever express 🙏

@SumantaTF @bhavyegoel @CenturionGuy1
